improvements.problem: The open loop 'And nobody turned it on for you' is intriguing but the follow-through ('no camera needed') is delivered as a soft text overlay, so the spoken track goes momentarily empty right where retention is most fragile.
improvements.suggestion: Put the paradox in the voice, not just the overlay: (0:05) 'Nobody turned it on — and it doesn't even use a camera.' Pair with the motion-wave B-roll pulsing on the word 'camera.'

improvements.problem: 'New routers track motion using the signal itself' is the most technical line in the script. At Reels pace it reads as abstract jargon and the curiosity dips before the creepy payoff at 0:15.
improvements.suggestion: Make it concrete and bodily faster: (0:10) 'The Wi-Fi signal bends around your body as you move.' Show a single clean diagram of a wave warping around a walking silhouette.

improvements.problem: The CTA 'Comment router and I'll show the setting' charges a comment-toll for the exact answer the video promised, which reads as engagement bait and softens the share trigger at the close.
improvements.suggestion: Give the gate a reason: (0:30) 'I can't show the exact setting here or this gets flagged. Comment router and I'll DM you.' This stacks a forbidden-knowledge hook onto the ask instead of withholding for clicks.
